title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: The Tate brothers fight extradition to the U.K. where they face rape and sex trafficking charges
stories.summary:
The Tate brothers fight extradition to the U.K. where they face rape and sex trafficking charges. Social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate will fight extradition to the United Kingdom where the brothers are accused of rape and sex trafficking, their lawyer said Monday, suggesting their arrest in Miami was politically motivated without explaining why.

Al Jazeera: Tate brothers vow to fight extradition as they appear at court in Miami. Al Jazeera reports that controversial ‘manosphere’ influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate appeared at a federal court hearing in Miami.

stories.title: Hamas Names Founding Member as Political Leader to Succeed Yahya Sinwar
stories.summary:
Hamas Names Founding Member as Political Leader to Succeed Yahya Sinwar. The Hamas terrorist organization on Monday named Khalil al-Hayya to become the head of its political bureau, taking the post previously held by Yahya Sinwar, the October 7 mastermind who was eliminated by an Israeli strike in July 2024.

The post Hamas Names Founding Member as Political Leader to Succeed Yahya Sinwar appeared first on <a. BBC: Hamas names Khalil al-Hayya as new overall leader.

BBC reports that the Palestinian armed group's Qatar-based chief negotiator replaces Yahya Sinwar, who was killed by Israeli forces in Gaza in 2024.

Trump Imposes 50 Percent Tariff on Variety of Canadian Goods Over 'Discrimination' of American Autos. President Donald Trump on Monday imposed a 50 percent duty on a variety of Canadian goods, including alcohol, dairy products, hockey sticks, and many others.

The post Trump Imposes 50 Percent Tariff on Variety of Canadian Goods Over ‘Discrimination’ of American Autos appeared first on <a rel="nofollow" href="https.

stories.title: What are the new British Prime Minister’s biggest challenges?
stories.summary:
What are the new British Prime Minister’s biggest challenges?. Britain has a new Prime Minister: Andy Burnham.

The former Mayor of Manchester must now unite a divided nation.

National Review: Andy Burnham Is Bad News for British Business. National Review reports that the incoming British prime minister shows an inexplicable nostalgia for the business-starving policies of the 1970s.

stories.title: Sinaloa Cartel Kingpin 'El Mayo' Sentenced in U.S. to Life in Prison
stories.summary:
Sinaloa Cartel Kingpin 'El Mayo' Sentenced in U.S. to Life in Prison. On Monday morning, Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada went before U.S. District Judge Brian Cogan, who handed down the life sentence.

The sentence was anticipated ahead of time following his August 2025 guilty plea, where the Mexican drug lord admitted to multiple drug trafficking conspiracy charges linked to his role as one of the founders and top leaders of the Sinaloa Cartel.

The post <a rel="nofollow" href="https://www.breitbart.com/border/2026/07/20/sinaloa-cartel-kingpin-el-mayo-sentenced-. BBC: Notorious drug cartel leader Ismael Zambada sentenced to life in US prison.

BBC reports that known as "El Mayo", the co-founder the Sinaloa Cartel was also ordered to pay $15bn after he admitted to sending vast amounts of drugs to the US.

stories.summary: Surgeon banned after 'catastrophic error' contributes to patient's death. stories.title: New footage shows Peter Falconio murderer Bradley John Murdoch refusing to reveal location – video
stories.summary:
New footage shows Peter Falconio murderer Bradley John Murdoch refusing to reveal location – video. Never-before-seen footage has been released by the Northern Territory police 25 years on from the murder of British backpacker Peter Falconio.

The body-worn footage shows officers attempting to get killer Bradley John Murdoch to confess and reveal the location of Falconio's body, weeks before his death in 2025.

Murdoch was convicted and sentenced to life in prison in 2005 over the murder of Falconio, a 28-year-old British backpacker and the assault and attempted kidnapping of his partner, Joa.

stories.summary: Hackers are exploiting recently patched WordPress bugs, putting millions of websites at risk. stories.title: Experts warn of rise in sadistic online exploitation of vulnerable children
stories.summary:
Experts warn of rise in sadistic online exploitation of vulnerable children. Global increase in cases of predators coercing children to harm themselves or produce child sexual abuse material Cases of predators coercing children into producing sexual photos and videos of themselves have surged globally in the past year, a cruel phenomenon known as “sadistic online exploitation”, child safety experts and law enforcement officials warn.

In cases of sadistic online exploitation, perpetrators target vulnerable teens on social media platforms and online games. T.

stories.title: My patients use ChatGPT for therapy. Now I use it too | Sarah Dargouth
stories.summary:
My patients use ChatGPT for therapy. Now I use it too | Sarah Dargouth.

I can’t blame my patients for turning to its straightforward assessments. But it has real risks – and care may require human messiness “Chat told me I should break up with him.” I instructed my face to remain therapist-neutral, but I must have smirked.

The truth is, I was annoyed. We had been discussing the viability of this relationship for weeks, and in an instant AI had brought the answer.

“How do you feel about it?” She said this had been her gut feeling all along. The followi.

stories.title: Doubt that Elon Musk ‘earned’ his trillion? Rightwing media says you’re in an ‘impotent envy cult’ | Arwa Mahdawi
stories.summary:
Doubt that Elon Musk ‘earned’ his trillion? Rightwing media says you’re in an ‘impotent envy cult’ | Arwa Mahdawi.

The lovefest from Musk’s conservative fans completely overlooks the unscrupulous tactics behind his immense wealth For 12 glorious days in June, Elon Musk experienced something nobody else in the history of humankind has ever experienced: trillionaire status.

Did all those zeros make Musk happy? Did the army of children he has sired love him more?

Did he find inner peace? We’ll never know b.

stories.summary:
AI-altered images on birdwatching forums putting research at risk. Experts warn increase in enhanced photos on birding platforms creating fake sightings, threatening credibility of tool used by scientists For many birdwatchers, recording a species outside its normal range is the holy grail.

In the UK, the discoveries often make national headlines. The western reef heron, for example, usually found in Africa and southern Europe, was spotted in a seasid.

stories.title: PNP tightens security as Uplift aid reaches millions of Filipinos
stories.summary:
PNP tightens security as Uplift aid reaches millions of Filipinos.

MANILA, Philippines — As millions of Filipinos receive government aid this week, the Philippine National Police (PNP) ordered heightened security measures nationwide for the expanded Unified Package for Livelihood, Industry, Food and Transport (Uplift) program.

The Department of Social Welfare and Development said the program serves as the latest round of financial assistance for individuals.

stories.summary:
MMDA: Don’t fall for ‘NCAP’ text scams. MANILA, Philippines — The Metropolitan Manila Development Authority (MMDA) on Monday warned motorists against falling for fraudulent text messages and fake websites exploiting the No Contact Apprehension Policy (NCAP).

The MMDA said scammers have been sending text messages claiming that the recipients’ alleged traffic fines are about to expire and threatening them with a 25-percent.

stories.summary:
Senate okays subpoena for VP’s tax, bank docs. MANILA, Philippines — The Senate impeachment court on Monday cleared a major hurdle for House prosecutors in Vice President Sara Duterte’s impeachment trial, authorizing subpoenas for her bank, tax and Anti-Money Laundering Council (AMLC) records that could provide key evidence for allegations she amassed unexplained wealth.

stories.title: Apple Crumble: a tasty Knives Out-inspired mystery that lets you snoop on your own family
stories.summary:
Apple Crumble: a tasty Knives Out-inspired mystery that lets you snoop on your own family. The developer of Duck Detective reveals details of her ‘sad grandma game’ follow-up: a whodunnit – or who-plans-to-do-it? – with a darker edge Someone wants to kill grandma.

In this Knives Out - inspired mystery game, each of your family members is suspicious in their own way: your stressed mum, your “midlife crisis uncle” and especially that stranger in your bedroom. Yikes!
